steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Transitions question. The blank connects two sentences: the first states that Arabic-speaking Muslim states ruled Al-Andalus for centuries; the second states that Arabic words entered Spanish. The blank must signal that the second sentence is a result of the first.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Consequently,’: Marks a cause-and-effect relationship. Centuries of Arabic rule caused Arabic words to enter Spanish — this is the logical connection. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Specifically,’: Signals that the second sentence narrows or specifies a category from the first. The first sentence describes a political fact, not a category of effects to specify. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘For example,’: Signals that the second sentence is one example of a broader point made in the first. The first sentence does not mention any broader set of outcomes for this to exemplify. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Instead,’: Signals a contrast or substitution. The second sentence does not contradict or replace the first — it follows from it. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
